title: Diplocaulobium pentanema
taxonomy: Diplocaulobium pentanema is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest and most diverse family of flowering plants. Diplocaulobium pentanema further belongs to the subtribe Malaxideae within the Orchidaceae family. This orchid is native to New Guinea, and its classification is further defined by its unique characteristics, including its growth habit and reproductive structures. Overall, the classification of Diplocaulobium pentanema reflects its distinct evolutionary history and its place within the larger orchid family.
uses: Diplocaulobium pentanema, also known as the Pentanemum orchid, is reported to have various medicinal properties in traditional medicine.
